channel.title: Apache-Iceberg on Indrajith Indraprastham
channel.description: Recent content in Apache-Iceberg on Indrajith Indraprastham

item.title: Distributed Dedup at Scale: The Redis Bloom Filter That Replaced 16 TB of RAM
item.description: A major North American retailer needed to process two billion point-of-sale files. The existing architecture couldn’t survive two thousand. This is the story of the rebuild, the two dead ends we hit along the way, and the one design decision that made the whole thing scale. The short version: the original pipeline wasn’t slow because the code was bad. It was slow because the architecture was the wrong shape for the problem.
